A dragon skull the size of a cathedral emerges from shifting desert sands, empty eye sockets dark as caverns, jawbone creating natural archway.
Ribcage of an ancient wyrm spans a misty valley, each bone thick as oak trunks, moss-covered vertebrae forming a bridge between cliffs.
Faded murals cover a canyon wall, depicting aerial battles between dragon flights, their painted scales still gleaming faintly in afternoon light.
A cavern floor glitters with centuries of unclaimed treasure, gold coins buried under dust, a single scholar's lamp illuminating forgotten wealth.
Five petrified dragon eggs rest in a volcanic crater nest, their crystallized shells refracting rainbow light, each larger than a wagon.
Ruined temple carved with dragon reliefs, massive stone statues flanking an altar where offerings still lie, undisturbed by time or thieves.
Clifftop aerie crumbles into the sea below, iron chains thick as tree trunks rust against stone, rotted leather saddles barely recognizable.
Forge chamber holds half-finished swords crafted from dragon bone, tools scattered as if smiths abandoned their sacred work mid-swing.
A vast chamber's walls are lined with shed dragon scales, each the size of shields, still shimmering with preserved iridescent magic.
The dragon graveyard stretches to the horizon, thousands of bleached skeletons rest in eternal silence, a place of pilgrimage and reverence.
A dragon frozen mid-roar in volcanic glass, entire body petrified during ancient eruption, now a monument towering over the scarred landscape.
Underground library preserves illuminated manuscripts cataloging dragon breeds, meticulous anatomical drawings, behavioral notes in fading ink on crumbling vellum.
Cliff face bears massive talon gouges from generations of nesting, scorched stone surrounds empty aeries, wind whistling through abandoned dragon nurseries.
Dragon spine fossilized into a stone ridge crossing barren plains, each vertebra worn smooth by centuries of wind and weather.
Crystal cavern contains dragon skeleton encased in translucent mineral growth, bones visible through sparkling formations like amber-trapped insects but colossal.
Ancient battlefield littered with dragon and rider bones intermingled, rusted weapons, broken banners, telling story of a forgotten war's final stand.
Monastery library's ceiling mural shows dragons teaching robed scholars, gilt letters naming lost disciplines, empty pedestals where sacred texts once rested.
Enormous tooth embedded in mountainside like a pillar, ivory surface carved with commemorative inscriptions in languages no longer spoken or remembered.
Dragon's petrified claw grips a boulder atop a plateau, each talon longer than spears, stone flesh preserving the creature's final moment.
Tidal cave reveals dragon bones only at lowest tide, barnacle-encrusted ribs creating cathedral arches, skulls housing populations of nesting seabirds.
Frost-preserved dragon lies in glacier ice, visible through blue transparency, scales intact, eyes closed as if merely sleeping through the ages.
Workshop contains intricate saddle-making tools, half-finished dragon harnesses sized for creatures vast as ships, leather patterns pinned to dust-covered workbenches.
Volcanic glass plain holds the shadow-impression of a dragon's final landing, wings spread wide, every detail burned into the cooled lava forever.
Mountain pass narrows through a dragon's fossilized coil, explorers walking through spaces between petrified ribs, vertebrae forming natural tunnel overhead.
Observatory dome's frescoes map dragon migration routes across painted stars, celestial charts marking seasonal flights that will never come again, telescopes aimed skyward.
